test.name: Evidence-quality question should route to record-extraction
test.description: User asks whether the informant on src_004 (James Brown, son-in-law) makes the father's-name entry primary or secondary information. That is an evidence-quality question for record-extraction, which owns three-layer classification since the assertion-classification merge (2026-07-11). The citation skill corrects terminology if it appears in a citation string but does not evaluate information weight.

input.user_message: Is the informant on src_004 primary or secondary for the father's name?

negative.explanation: Assessing whether information on a record is primary or secondary based on informant proximity is evidence classification — record-extraction's job (classification is first-and-final at extraction and refined there on request; the former assertion-classification skill was merged into it). The citation skill corrects source-vs-information terminology when it appears in a citation string it is formatting, but does not evaluate the weight or quality of information on a source.

judge_context: Should route to record-extraction, the classification owner since the assertion-classification merge
May gently note the terminology distinction (primary/secondary applies to information, not sources) but must still redirect
Should NOT attempt to answer the primary/secondary question itself
